summary: Raw MSI data are exported as plain-text CSV files from standard MSI software such as SCiLS or MSiReader.
In the mass2adduct CSV format, columns represent mass peaks with m/z values as column names, and rows represent pixels.
Entries in the mass2adduct data table represent intensity values for a given peak and pixel.
The massdiff function returns an object of classes data.frame and massdiff.
In the massdiff output, column A represents the parent ion peak mass. In the massdiff output, column B represents the putative adduct ion peak mass (higher than A).
In the massdiff output, column diff is the difference between A and B.
corrPairsMSI performs a correlation test on each pair of peaks in a massdiff object.
corrPairsMSI uses the two-tailed Pearson method by default for correlation testing.
The how=parallel option can speed up corrPairsMSI processing on computers with multiple processors.
The ncores parameter in corrPairsMSI specifies the number of processors to use.
Correlation test results in corrPairsMSI output include Estimate (correlation coefficient), P.value, and Significance.
The Significance column in corrPairsMSI output indicates whether the p-value is below the threshold after Bonferroni correction.
Raw p-values are reported in corrPairsMSI output without Bonferroni correction for user-conducted analysis.
